title: Applications should not create session cookies from untrusted input
description: Session Cookie Injection occurs when a web application assigns session cookies to users using untrusted data. Session cookies are used by web applications to identify users. Thus, controlling these enable control over the identity of the users within the application.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant: // Use secure authentication instead of session cookies

checks.message: Applications should not create session cookies from untrusted sources.
